title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
The Fetch API is the modern, Promise-based way to make HTTP requests from the browser (and Node.js 18+).

How fetch works:
1. fetch(url, options) returns a Promise that resolves to a Response object.
2. Check response.ok (true for 2xx status codes).
3. Parse the body: response.json(), response.text(), response.blob().
4. The Response body can only be consumed ONCE.

fetch does NOT reject on HTTP errors (404, 500) — it only rejects on network failures. You must check response.ok manually.

For POST requests, pass method, headers, and body in the options object.
